summary: Mixed‑quality recording – a crisp first set with standout Candyman and Esau, but a second set so muddy it drowns out the performance.
review: The release contains two very different sources. Set 1 sounds clean and full‑range (thanks to a Nagra‑300 style capture), delivering strong versions of Candyman, Esau and Don't Ease Me In with Garcia’s playful nuances and solid bass rumble from Phil. Set 2 is plagued by heavy mud and muffling, making even the hot China>Rider performance hard to enjoy. Overall the show is decent musically but the recording inconsistency limits its appeal.

ai_rating.rationale: Show performance scores are around 3.9/5 while recording quality averages about 2.3/5; weighted combination yields ~3.4 stars. Two detailed reviews give a moderate confidence level.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Set 1 is clear with slight high‑end boost; Set 2 is extremely muddy and muffled, sounding as if recorded through a towel over the mics.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and varied with strong song choices; second set is more basic and its impact is marred by poor audio, though the China>Rider transition is noted as a hot performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia adds playful vocal nuances on Candyman and delivers solid lead work throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass rumblings are captured nicely in the first set thanks to the high‑quality source.
